user.title: Set up Aurora
user.description: Aurora is your smart alarm clock. Setup is one click — there is nothing to configure here. Afterwards, open the Aurora app (the sidebar panel or the dashboard card) to bind your devices, set shared options and add alarms.

abort.single_instance_allowed: Aurora is already configured. Only one installation is supported — manage everything inside the Aurora app.

update_alarm.name: Update alarm
update_alarm.description: Update fields of an existing alarm.

id.name: Alarm ID
id.description: The id of the alarm to update.

id.name: Alarm ID
id.description: The id of the alarm to remove.

skip_next.name: Skip next
skip_next.description: Skip the next occurrence of an alarm.

id.name: Alarm ID
id.description: The id of the alarm to skip.

id.name: Alarm ID
id.description: Optional: which alarm to ring (defaults to the next/first).

speak_briefing.name: Speak briefing
speak_briefing.description: Compose and speak the wake-up briefing now (time, weather, calendar, to-dos).

id.name: Alarm ID
id.description: Optional: which alarm's briefing to speak (defaults to the active/next/first).

benchmark_vision.name: Benchmark vision
benchmark_vision.description: Run timed AI-vision inferences to measure latency on your provider/hardware.

not_setup.message: Aurora is not set up yet.

no_alarm_to_trigger.message: No alarm is available to trigger.

no_alarm_for_briefing.message: No alarm is available for a briefing.

no_audio_sink.title: Aurora has no speaker configured
no_audio_sink.description: One or more alarms are enabled, but no speaker (AudioSink role) is bound — alarms will only show a notification and won't play a sound. Open the Aurora app → Devices and bind a media player to play the ringtone.
